question: [File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / openTrove / _withdrawUSDS] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (USDSGasCompensation set to very large value e.g. 1e24 via timelock, no upper bound in _setUSDSGasCompensation) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setUSDSGasCompensation(1e24) → borrower calls openTrove with _USDSAmount=minNetDebt → compositeDebt=minNetDebt+1e24 → ICR check uses compositeDebt → borrower must provide enormous collateral → if they do, 1e24 USDS minted to GasPool), violating INVARIANT (USDSGasCompensation must be bounded to prevent minting unbounded USDS to GasPool), causing scoped impact: if a borrower opens a trove with the new large gas comp, 1e24 USDS is minted to GasPool and can be claimed by any liquidator as gas compensation, effectively minting USDS from nothing? Local proof idea: unit test with USDSGasCompensation=1e24, assert GasPool USDS balance is bounded and usdsToken.totalSupply() remains consistent.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMCR / _requireICRisAboveMCR / withdrawColl] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (MCR lowered via timelock from 110% to 101%, borrower has trove with ICR=105%) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setMCR(1.01e18) → borrower calls withdrawColl to reduce ICR to 102% → _requireICRisAboveMCR checks newICR >= 1.01e18 ✓ → borrower withdraws collateral that would have been blocked under original MCR), violating INVARIANT (collateral withdrawal must not reduce ICR below the original MCR that was in effect at trove creation), causing scoped impact: borrower extracts BNB collateral at ICR=102% that was previously locked, leaving trove undercollateralized relative to original protocol parameters? Local proof idea: unit test with MCR change, assert withdrawColl is blocked for ICR between new and old MCR.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMCR / _requireICRisAboveMCR / withdrawUSDS] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (MCR lowered via timelock, borrower has trove with ICR=105% which was previously unable to borrow more) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setMCR(1.01e18) → borrower calls withdrawUSDS → _requireICRisAboveMCR checks newICR >= 1.01e18 → borrower mints additional USDS reducing ICR to 102%), violating INVARIANT (USDS minting must not reduce ICR below the original MCR), causing scoped impact: borrower extracts additional USDS debt at ICR that was previously blocked, increasing protocol insolvency risk? Local proof idea: unit test with MCR change, assert withdrawUSDS is blocked for resulting ICR between new and old MCR.
